title: Footnote: The Compiler Designer's Handbook
subtitle: A Modern Compendium for Compiler Design and Implementation
author: Hassan Ernest
themes: The full lifecycle of compilation from source to executable
Layered architecture: front end, middle end, and back end
Formal language theory as the foundation of lexical and syntactic analysis
Theory and practice of code optimization across all levels
Runtime systems and their inseparable relationship to compiled code
Modern compiler infrastructure: LLVM, MLIR, and compiler generators
The trade-off between compile-time analysis depth and runtime performance
Advanced techniques: JIT compilation, AOT, profile-guided optimization, and LTO
Compiler design as a unified engineering discipline combining algorithms, data structures, and machine architecture
excerpt: Footnote: The Compiler Designer's Handbook is a modern, self-contained compendium that walks the reader through every stage of compiler construction — from scanning tokens to generating optimized machine code — while bridging classical theory with today's production infrastructure. Hassan Ernest brings together formal language theory, practical algorithm design, and real-world tooling to produce the definitive reference for the working compiler engineer.

relatedBooks.title: Introduction to the Theory of Computation
